title: viola canina subsp. canina
intro: Viola canina subsp. canina, also known as Heath Dog Violet or Wood Violet, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, pale purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. canina include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
